top_stories.title: Idira launches as Palo Alto Networks extends CyberArk tech to machine and agentic identities
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks launched Idira, a new identity security platform that manages human, machine, and AI agent identities under a single PAM framework. The platform incorporates CyberArk technology to extend privileged access management capabilities to machine and agentic identities across the enterprise.

top_stories.title: Over 70% of organizations hit by identity breaches
top_stories.summary: A Sophos survey of 5,000 IT and cybersecurity leaders across 17 countries found that over 70% of organizations experienced identity breaches. Attackers primarily used stolen credentials, compromised service accounts, and social engineering tactics targeting employees, according to the State of Identity Security 2026 report.

top_stories.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
top_stories.summary: Google disclosed that a threat actor used an AI-developed zero-day exploit to bypass two-factor authentication (2FA), marking the first confirmed instance of AI being used in the wild for malicious vulnerability discovery and exploit generation. The attack was attributed to cybercrime threat actors and represents a novel threat vector directly targeting multi-factor authentication mechanisms.

top_stories.title: Identity security firm SailPoint discloses GitHub repository breach
top_stories.summary: SailPoint disclosed a breach of its GitHub repository on April 20, stating the incident has been contained. The company reported that no customer data was affected by the breach.

top_stories.title: Machine identities outnumber humans 109 to 1
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks' 2026 Identity Security Landscape report finds organizations manage an average of 109 machine identities for every human identity. AI agent identities are projected to grow 85% over the next 12 months, with total machine identities expected to increase 77% and human identities 56%.

news.title: Microsoft’s agentic security system found four critical Windows RCE flaws
news.summary: Microsoft announced its agentic AI security system, codenamed MDASH, discovered 16 new vulnerabilities in the Windows networking and authentication stack, including four critical remote code execution flaws. Two of the critical CVEs — CVE-2026-40361 and CVE-2026-40364 — were flagged by Microsoft as more likely to be exploited.

news.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities, including 30 rated Critical, covering flaws in DNS and Netlogon among other products. Of the 138 vulnerabilities, 61 are privilege escalation bugs, which are directly relevant to access control and identity security.

news.title: Fortinet warns of critical RCE flaws in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator
news.summary: Fortinet has released security patches addressing two critical vulnerabilities in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator that could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code or commands. FortiAuthenticator is Fortinet's multi-factor authentication and identity management product, making this directly relevant to IAM deployments.

news.title: Two-Thirds of Enterprises Suspect AI Agents Have Already Accessed Unauthorized Data, Akeyless Finds
news.summary: A global study by Akeyless found that two-thirds of enterprises using AI agents suspect those agents have already accessed unauthorized data. Most organizations cannot detect compromised AI agents for hours, and are spending over $1 million managing the resulting fallout.

news.title: Saviynt Strengthens Executive Team to Accelerate AI Identity Security Leadership
news.summary: Saviynt has appointed Pete Angstadt as its first Chief Commercial Officer and Brad Myers as Chief Customer Officer. Both are described as industry veterans joining the IAM/identity security vendor's executive team.

news.title: Are You Using the Right Security Controls for Agent 365?
news.summary: Microsoft's Agent 365 platform integrates with Microsoft Entra as part of its security and compliance framework for managing AI agents. The platform combines identity and access controls through Microsoft Entra alongside Microsoft Purview and Microsoft Defender to enforce security standards for AI agent management.

news.title: Descope Named to Rising in Cyber 2026 List of Top Cybersecurity Startups
news.summary: Descope, a customer identity and access management (CIAM) platform, has been named to the Rising in Cyber 2026 list of top cybersecurity startups, voted on by CISOs. The list recognizes 30 private cybersecurity companies, with Descope selected for its work in identity and authentication.

news.title: How to use streamlined permissions for Amazon S3 Tables and Iceberg materialized views
news.summary: AWS published a guide on configuring access controls for Amazon S3 Tables and Iceberg materialized views using IAM-based authorization. The article covers setting up S3 Tables in the AWS Glue Data Catalog and managing permissions across an analytics stack.

news.title: VU#937808: Casdoor contains Arbitrary File Write vulnerability
news.summary: Casdoor, an open-source identity and access management platform, contains an arbitrary file write vulnerability (VU#937808) in its "Local File System" storage provider implementation. An authenticated user with file upload permissions can exploit insufficient path sanitization to write files outside the intended directory boundaries.

news.title: Attackers targeting storage infrastructure for remote work
news.summary: Threat actors are increasingly targeting storage infrastructure by exploiting identity, remote work, and zero trust gaps to cripple backups and leverage cloud misconfigurations. The article frames ransomware attacks in the context of identity and zero trust weaknesses as contributing factors.

news.title: SEON Appoints Sagnik Nandy, CTO of Docusign, to Board of Directors
news.summary: SEON, a fraud prevention company, has appointed Sagnik Nandy to its board of directors. Nandy is the current CTO of Docusign and a former engineering leader at both Google and Okta.

news.title: Europe wants out from under US tech – but first it has to find the exits
news.summary: A report has mapped Europe's dependencies on US technology across cloud infrastructure, identity systems, and public sector procurement. The analysis identifies weak points where European organizations rely on American vendors for identity and related services.

news.title: Hackers Trick DigiCert Into Issuing Certificates Used to Sign Malware
news.summary: DigiCert revoked 60 code signing certificates after hackers used a malicious support chat attachment to trick the company into issuing certificates used to sign Zhong Stealer malware. The incident involved social engineering of DigiCert's support process to obtain legitimate code signing certificates, which were then used to make malware appear trusted.

news.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Linux backdoor called PamDOORa that abuses Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) to steal SSH credentials and maintain persistent access. The toolkit, sold for $1,600 on a Russian cybercrime forum, uses a magic password and TCP port combination to bypass normal authentication controls on compromised Linux systems.

news.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
news.summary: Attackers are deploying the CloudZ RAT and a new plugin called Pheno to abuse the Windows Phone Link application, enabling theft of SMS messages from connected smartphones. This attack vector allows threat actors to intercept SMS-based one-time passwords, effectively bypassing SMS-based two-factor authentication.

news.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
news.summary: A proof-of-concept exploit demonstrates that Microsoft Edge stores passwords in process memory, allowing someone with administrative privileges to extract stored credentials. The vulnerability highlights risks for enterprise environments where credential theft could enable further lateral movement or privilege escalation.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The charges relate to SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in cryptocurrency theft from investors.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens at scale. The campaign compromised over 18,000 networks by silently stealing authentication tokens without deploying malware.
